questions.question: Explain why the Central Limit Theorem is critically important when comparing the means of two large, independent samples.
questions.modelAnswer: The CLT states that the sampling distribution of the sample mean will be approximately normal for large sample sizes (n > 30), regardless of the population's distribution. This normality allows us to use the standard normal (Z) distribution to construct confidence intervals and perform hypothesis tests on the difference between two population means. It justifies the use of the two-sample Z-test formula, which relies on the standard error of the difference between two independent sample means.

questions.question: A data scientist is comparing the average daily usage time (in minutes) of two different versions of a mobile app (Version A and Version B) using large samples. State the null and alternative hypotheses for a test to determine if there is a significant difference between the two versions. Then, list the two primary ways this hypothesis test can be used for statistical inference.
questions.modelAnswer: Null Hypothesis (H₀): μ_A = μ_B (There is no difference in the average daily usage time between Version A and Version B). Alternative Hypothesis (H₁): μ_A ≠ μ_B (There is a significant difference in the average daily usage time). The test can be used for: 1) Calculating a confidence interval for the difference in population means (μ_A - μ_B). 2) Calculating a p-value to test the null hypothesis against the alternative.

questions.question: Describe the step-by-step process for constructing a 95% confidence interval for the difference between two population means (μ₁ - μ₂) using large, independent samples. Your answer should include the formula and an explanation of what the interval means.
questions.modelAnswer: 1. Calculate the sample means (X̄₁, X̄₂) and sample standard deviations (s₁, s₂). 2. Compute the standard error: SE = √(s₁²/n₁ + s₂²/n₂). 3. Find the critical z-value for 95% confidence (z* ≈ 1.96). 4. Calculate the margin of error: ME = z* × SE. 5. Construct the interval: (X̄₁ - X̄₂) ± ME. The interval means we are 95% confident that the true difference in population means lies within this range. If 0 is not in the interval, it suggests a statistically significant difference.
